steps.prompt: This phrase has no finite verb. What verb is implied?

hints.est (is): 'est' is singular, but 'beati' and 'pauperes' are plural — 'sunt' is needed
hints.erunt (will be): The implied verb is present tense 'sunt', not future 'erunt'
hints.erant (were): The implied verb is present tense 'sunt', not imperfect 'erant'

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would be 'beati' (same form), but here it is nominative plural
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'beati', but this is a declarative statement
hints.dative: Dative singular would be 'beato' — 'beati' is nominative plural

hints.accusative: Accusative plural is also 'pauperes' (same form), but here it is the subject
hints.vocative: Vocative plural would also be 'pauperes', but this is a declaration, not an address
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'pauperibus' — 'pauperes' is nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'pauperis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'pauperis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'pauperis' ends in -is
